description_long: This object is a brown chicken egg that has been cracked open, with two large shell halves resting on a glossy, slightly reflective surface. The shell is a rich brown color with a smooth, hard exterior and a thin, jagged edge where it has broken. The interior of the shell is lighter, almost off-white, and the broken edges are irregular and sharp. There is a small pool of translucent egg white beneath the shell, suggesting the egg has been freshly cracked. The shell halves are hollow, with the inner surfaces visible and showing a subtle texture. No yolk is present, indicating it has either been removed or is out of view.
description: A cracked brown chicken egg with two shell halves resting on a glossy surface and a small pool of egg white beneath. The shell is smooth and hard, with jagged broken edges and a lighter interior.
description_view_0: The egg is seen split into two main halves with jagged edges, both resting on a glossy surface with visible egg white beneath.
description_view_1: The unbroken, rounded exterior of the egg is visible, showing a smooth, brown surface with no cracks apparent from this angle.
description_view_2: The cracked shell is viewed from the opposite side, emphasizing the hollow interior and the irregular, broken edges of both halves.
description_view_3: The inside of one shell half is prominently visible, showing the lighter, textured interior and the broken edge, with the other half partially obscured.
attribute_orientation: lying on its side with the broken edges facing upward and outward
attribute_contextual_placement: Most likely encountered as part of food preparation, such as during baking or cooking, after being cracked open to use the contents.
